alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a subset of only high-ICC features produced superior Rank-1-Identification Rate (Rank-1-IR) performance in 9 of 10 databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selecting the most stable features based on the ICC resulted in the best biometric performance in 12 of 14 databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same phenomenon but with more databases and a stronger aggregated result (12/14 vs 9/10), so the claim has been revised with broader evidence and a stronger outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a subset of only high-ICC features produced superior performance in Equal Error Rate (EER) in 8 of 10 databases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a subset of only high-ICC features produced superior Equal Error Rate (EER) performance in 12 of 14 databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication updates the numeric evidence (more databases and a higher success count) and increases certainty, so the result is revised, broadened, and more strongly stated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prescreening potential biometric features and choosing only highly reliable features will yield better performance than lower ICC features or than the set of all features combined. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: As the ICC of a group of features increases, the median of the genuine similarity score distribution increases and the spread of this distribution decreases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a specific empirical mechanism (changes in genuine score distribution as ICC increases) that supports the preprint's general conclusion about prescreening, so the general claim is clarified by mechanistic results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best way to assess temporal persistence is to perform a test-retest study and assess test-retest reliability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC) is introduced as an index of the temporal persistence of a single biometric feature. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication operationalizes the preprint recommendation by introducing ICC as a concrete index for test-retest reliability, increasing specificity and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach would likely be applicable to any biometric modality where the features can be expressed as quantitative values on an interval or ratio scale. (certainty=low, type=hypothesis)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of reliable features, as measured by ICC, allowed for achieving an authentication performance with EER = 2.01% in eye movement-driven biometrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad, low-certainty hypothesis about cross-modality applicability is replaced in the publication by a concrete, high-certainty demonstration in a single modality (eye movement), narrowing the scope and amplifying confidence for that specific case.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel spectral graph technique of locally-biased semi-supervised eigenvectors can characterize the natural variations in observed spectra data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel spectral technique of locally-biased semi-supervised eigenvectors yields more reliable results for many astronomical questions compared to traditional PCA.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as providing superior, more reliable results across many astronomical questions (a comparative/stronger claim) rather than simply characterizing natural spectral variations, so the claim content has been revised and amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived embeddings of spectra from the SDSS Main Galaxy Sample carry an unprecedented amount of information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The derived spectral coordinates from the method applied to the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Main Galaxy Sample carry an unprecedented amount of information.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this claim essentially verbatim about the SDSS Main Galaxy Sample embeddings, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The main features of the spectra correlate strongly with star formation rate and clearly separate active galactic nuclei.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The global variant of the proposed approach can identify numerous astronomical phenomena of interest with high precision.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's specific findings (star-formation rate and AGN separation) into a broader claim about identifying many phenomena, dropping the specific correlations, so the original is revised and the specificity is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can enable new discoveries in the data and provide a detailed understanding of very fine local structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The locally-biased variants of the approach allow for the exploration of subtle trends around a set of chosen objects.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that locally-biased variants enable exploration of subtle, local trends around chosen objects, which clarifies and narrows the preprint's broader claim about discovering fine local structure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Estimates of the information divergence over the parametric class of multinomial models lead to structure discovery methods whose mean squared error achieves parametric convergence rates as the sample size grows.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Estimates of the information divergence over the parametric class of multinomial models lead to structure discovery methods whose mean squared error achieves parametric convergence rates as the sample size grows.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical result verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A naive application of the method to continuous nonparametric multivariate models converges much more slowly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A naive application of the method to continuous nonparametric multivariate models converges much more slowly.
alignments.rationale: The claim appears unchanged between versions, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method for nonparametric structure discovery uses weighted ensemble divergence estimators that achieve parametric convergence rates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for nonparametric structure discovery uses weighted ensemble divergence estimators that achieve parametric convergence rates.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method obeys an asymptotic central limit theorem that facilitates hypothesis testing and other types of statistical validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method obeys an asymptotic central limit theorem that facilitates hypothesis testing and other types of statistical validation.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this theoretical claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for salient object detection has a closed form global optimum that estimates the salient object.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for salient object detection has a closed form global optimum solution.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the closed-form global optimum result with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides approximations to the global optimum for a criterion that integrates local/global contrast and large area saliency cues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides approximations to the global optimum for a criterion that integrates local/global contrast and large area saliency cues.
alignments.rationale: This technical description is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves mostly leading performance compared to state-of-the-art algorithms across a large set of salient object detection datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised approach achieves leading performance compared to state-of-the-art unsupervised algorithms across a large set of datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the approach is unsupervised and compares only to unsupervised algorithms (narrower), and tightens language to 'leading' rather than 'mostly leading,' which tempers the original broad claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational complexity of the proposed method is favorable/comparable to many state-of-the-art techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational complexity of the proposed method is favorable compared to many state-of-the-art unsupervised techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the comparison to focus on unsupervised techniques, narrowing the original broader complexity claim while keeping similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a formal framework for graph aggregation grounded in social choice theory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces a formal framework for graph aggregation grounded in social choice theory.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution statement verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study focuses on understanding which properties shared by individual input graphs will transfer to the output graph returned by a given aggregation rule.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study focuses on understanding which properties shared by individual input graphs will transfer to the output graph returned by a given aggregation rule.
alignments.rationale: This focus is preserved exactly in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results establish connections between the types of properties preserved under aggregation and the choice-theoretic axioms satisfied by the rules used.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results establish connections between the types of properties preserved under aggregation and the choice-theoretic axioms satisfied by the rules used.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most important result is a powerful impossibility theorem that generalises Arrow's seminal result for the aggregation of preference orders to a large collection of different types of graphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most important result is a powerful impossibility theorem that generalises Arrow's seminal result for the aggregation of preference orders to a large collection of different types of graphs.
alignments.rationale: The central impossibility theorem is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational cost of performing exact Bayesian inference over causal models grows rapidly as the number of relata increases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cognitive processes underlying causal learning must be substantially approximate due to the rapid growth of computational cost in performing exact Bayesian inference over causal models as the number of relata increases.
alignments.rationale: The publication embeds the empirical/formal cost-growth observation into a broader cognitive conclusion; the original technical observation is reframed as support for a cognitive claim, so the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognitive processes underlying causal learning must be substantially approximate due to computational constraints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cognitive processes underlying causal learning must be substantially approximate due to the rapid growth of computational cost in performing exact Bayesian inference over causal models as the number of relata increases.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears verbatim in the publication, unchanged in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose an algorithmic-level model of causal structure learning where learners represent only a single global hypothesis that they update locally as they gather evidence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose an algorithmic-level model of causal structure learning where learners represent only a single global hypothesis that they update locally as they gather evidence.
alignments.rationale: The algorithmic-level model claim is carried into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learners choose informative interventions that manipulate the causal system to help elucidate its workings under the limitations of their cognitive processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learners choose informative interventions that manipulate the causal system to help elucidate its workings under the limitations of their cognitive processes.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about intervention choice is preserved verbatim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach is supported by the analysis of four experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach is supported by the analysis of 3 experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication reduces the reported number of supporting experiments from four to three, revising the empirical support and tempering the strength of the evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach to stride length estimation using deep convolutional neural networks achieves an average accuracy and precision of 0.01 ± 5.37 cm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for stride length estimation using deep convolutional neural networks achieves high accuracy and precision.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of high accuracy/precision but omits the specific numeric result, which is a revision that weakens the quantitative specificity and thus tempers confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The precision of the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods evaluated on the benchmark dataset by 3.0 cm (36%).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods evaluated on the same benchmark dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the outperforming claim but drops the quantified margin (3.0 cm / 36%), revising the strength of the result and tempering its precision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is not subject to the methodological constraints that limit the applicability of state-of-the-art double integration methods due to its independence of stride definition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The independence of stride definition allows the proposed method to overcome methodological constraints of state-of-the-art double integration methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With more precise mobile stride length estimation, new insights into the progression of neurological disease or early indications might be gained.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improved precision in mobile stride length estimation may provide new insights into the progression of neurological diseases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for the investigation of previously uncharted diseases in terms of mobile gait analysis by re-training and applying the model.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this claim about enabling investigation of previously uncharted diseases via retraining, so the claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, Semi-Supervised Sparse Representation based Classification (S$^3$RC), addresses the challenge of face recognition with few or single labeled examples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes a variation dictionary to characterize linear nuisance variables through a sparsity framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method effectively addresses the challenges posed by both linear and non-linear nuisance variables in face recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's focus on linear nuisance variables to claim handling of both linear and non-linear nuisances, broadening and strengthening the original technical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves performance over existing methods on various face recognition databases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments conducted on the AR, Multi-PIE, CAS-PEAL, and LFW databases validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports experimental validation on specific databases, supporting the preprint's claim of significant performance improvement; meaning is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is effective even when there is only a single labeled sample per person.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, semi-supervised sparse representation-based classification, significantly improves face recognition performance with few or even a single labeled example.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly highlights performance even for a single labeled example with high certainty, which amplifies the preprint's medium-confidence claim of effectiveness in that setting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best policy obtained from a DESPOT is near-optimal, with a regret bound that depends on the representation size of the optimal policy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best policy obtained from a DESPOT is near-optimal, with a regret bound that depends on the representation size of the optimal policy.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the theoretical near-optimality and regret-bound result verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrates strong experimental results, compared with some of the best online POMDP algorithms available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrates strong experimental results compared with some of the best online POMDP algorithms available.
alignments.rationale: Experimental-strength claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm has been incorporated into an autonomous driving system for real-time vehicle control.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm has been incorporated into an autonomous driving system for real-time vehicle control.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of integration into an autonomous driving system is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SupCP generalizes the supervised singular value decomposition (SupSVD) for vector-valued observations to allow for observations that have the form of a matrix or higher-order array.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SupCP generalizes the supervised singular value decomposition (SupSVD) for vector-valued observations to allow for observations that have the form of a matrix or higher-order array.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SupCP can be used for dimension reduction, capturing latent structures that are more accurate and interpretable due to covariate supervision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SupCP specifies a full probability distribution for a multiway data observation with given covariate values, which can be used for predictive modeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SupCP specifies a full probability distribution for a multiway data observation with given covariate values, which can be used for predictive modeling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We give conditions for identifiability and develop an EM algorithm for simultaneous estimation of all model parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We give conditions for identifiability and develop an EM algorithm for simultaneous estimation of all model parameters.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contributions (identifiability conditions and EM algorithm) are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reactive multi-context systems (rMCSs) are well-suited for reactive reasoning in the presence of heterogeneous knowledge sources and data streams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reactive multi-context systems (rMCSs) are well-suited for reactive reasoning in the presence of heterogeneous knowledge sources and data streams.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: rMCSs can handle typical problems arising in the context of stream reasoning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: rMCSs can handle typical problems arising in the context of stream reasoning.
alignments.rationale: The claim about handling stream-reasoning problems is preserved directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inconsistencies occurring in the integration of multiple knowledge sources can be managed using rMCSs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inconsistencies occurring in the integration of multiple knowledge sources can be managed using rMCSs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The potential non-determinism of rMCSs can be avoided using an alternative, more skeptical well-founded semantics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The potential non-determinism of rMCSs can be avoided using an alternative, more skeptical well-founded semantics.

alignments.pub_claim_text: rMCSs generalize managed multi-context systems (mMCSs) to dynamic settings and extend relevant approaches in knowledge representation and stream reasoning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: New inverse optimization models and supporting algorithms can learn the parameters of heterogeneous travelers’ route behavior to infer shared network state parameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The inferred values from the new models are consistent with observations of each agent’s optimization behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using deteriorated wind measures negatively impacts the performance of wind power forecasts. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using deteriorated wind measures negatively impacts the performance of wind power forecasts. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding about degraded wind measures affecting forecast performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The default methodology for selecting a subset of predictors in the standard random forest package can be refined when there is a variable with a major impact. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The default methodology for selecting a subset of predictors in the standard random forest package can be refined when there is a variable with a major impact. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation about refining the predictor-selection methodology is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed robust regression approach effectively estimates the background intensity surface with minimal effects from foreground intensities and noises. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed robust regression approach effectively estimates the background intensity surface with minimal effects from foreground intensities and observation noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about robust background estimation with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves effective image binarization under significant background variations and observation noises. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves binary images of the foreground by subtracting the estimated background from the input image and applying global thresholding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the operational step (background subtraction + global thresholding) by which binarization is achieved, clarifying the preprint's effectiveness claim without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The outcomes of the proposed approach were validated using 26 test images and compared with nine existing image binarization methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms nine existing image binarization methods when validated using 26 test images and their corresponding ground truths. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the validation claim by asserting outperforming results and referencing ground truths, and it raises the stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining the proposed approach with three state-of-the-art morphological segmentation methods improves their image segmentation outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the proposed approach with three morphological segmentation methods improves their image segmentation outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim that combination improves segmentation outcomes is maintained; wording changes are minor and certainty is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioLeaf achieves foliar damage quantification with precision comparable to that of human specialists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioLeaf achieves foliar damage quantification with precision comparable to that of human specialists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed image processing methodology can assist soybean producers by reducing the time to measure foliar damage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image processing methodology can assist soybean producers by reducing the time to measure foliar damage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The assistive, time-saving conclusion for soybean producers is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology developed is applicable not only to soy but also to different crops such as cotton, bean, potato, coffee, and vegetables. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology developed can be applied to different crops such as cotton, bean, potato, coffee, and vegetables. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed mid-level attribute in the form of multidimensional template using Local Steering Kernel effectively addresses intrinsic image noise and pixel level uncertainty in thermal infrared images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LSK is designed to handle intrinsic image noise and pixel level uncertainty effectively by capturing local image geometry.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a new image similarity kernel in the maximum margin framework of support vector machines leads to a shorter and simpler training phase for pedestrian detection.

alignments.rationale: The content is the same (short/simple training due to a new kernel) but the publication lowers the stated certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Replacing the traditional sliding window detection methodology with multichannel discrete Fourier transform significantly improves the speed and efficiency of pedestrian localization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LSK templates enable exact acceleration of the sliding window-based detection methodology, resulting in very fast and efficient pedestrian localization.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert much faster localization, but the preprint emphasized a DFT-based replacement while the publication frames acceleration via LSK templates; the underlying method description is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental studies on publicly available thermal infrared images support the effectiveness of the proposed methods and model assumptions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mid-level attribute in the form of the multidimensional template, or tensor, using local steering kernel (LSK) improves pedestrian detection in far infrared images.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a concrete effectiveness claim about improved detection supported by experiments; this is consistent with the preprint's experimental support but expressed more strongly (amplified confidence).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The research includes the release of in-house annotations of pedestrians in over 17,000 frames of the OSU color thermal database for community use.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same data-release claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several popular multi-label algorithms are easily applicable to sequencing tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several popular multi-label algorithms are easily applicable to sequencing tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result claim verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study detects and elaborates on connections between multi-label methods and Markovian models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study detects and elaborates on connections between multi-label methods and Markovian models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel approaches developed in this study are competitive against established methods for sequential prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approaches developed in this study prove very competitive against established methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the competitiveness claim, with similar strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper investigates the suitability of multi-label methods for prediction in sequential data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper investigates the suitability of multi-label methods for prediction in sequential data.
alignments.rationale: The investigatory conclusion is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed recommendation algorithm effectively utilizes similarity information among users and items to improve predictions in sparse user-item rating matrices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed recommendation algorithm effectively utilizes similarity information among users and items to improve predictions in sparse user-item rating matrices.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new representation constructed by the proposed method preserves affinity and structural information in the user-item rating matrix.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new representation constructed by the proposed method preserves affinity and structural information in the user-item rating matrix.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of manifold learning in the proposed method enforces smoothness on the graphs constructed for users and items, capturing their proximities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive empirical evaluations on six benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed recommendation approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive empirical evaluations on six benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed recommendation approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper develops a novel categorical subspace learning approach to unravel the latent structure for Probit, Tobit, and Logit models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel categorical subspace learning approach is developed to unravel the latent structure for Probit, Tobit, and Logit models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method leads to lightweight first-order algorithms with highly parallelizable tasks per iteration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed rank regularized maximum-likelihood estimator is solved recursively via alternating majorization-minimization to sketch high-dimensional categorical data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Asymptotic convergence to the stationary point set of the batch estimator is established for infinite data streams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the subspace iterates shows asymptotic convergence to the stationary point set of the batch estimator for infinite data streams.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Sublinear regret bounds are derived for the empirical cost when analyzing finite data streams.
alignments.rationale: The result is the same, but the publication lists the certainty as medium rather than high, so confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulated tests corroborate the merits of the novel schemes for real-time movie recommendation and chess-game classification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a method for autonomous MAV navigation and exploration using a low-cost consumer-grade quadrocopter equipped with a monocular camera.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, sparse principal component regression for generalized linear models (SPCR-glm), enables the simultaneous consideration of regression loss and PCA loss. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method uses semantically-aware patches with adaptive size and location to overcome classical processing limits. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach combines semantic non-rigid patches, high-quality prior data, and non-rigid registration tools to solve the blind inverse problem. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes semantic non-rigid patches and registration tools consistent with the preprint's semantically-aware adaptive patches, so the contribution is preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is effective for enhancing the quality of dark facial images across different identities, expressions, and poses. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mechanistic emulation may be favorable for extrapolation in time and dealing with sparse and unevenly sampled data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fast surrogate models, or emulators, provide a solution to the efficiency demand in model-based real-time control of urban water infrastructures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resistive PCM devices show a two-fold to ten-fold reduction in error rate in a missing pixel pattern completion task trained over 30 epochs, compared to the untrained case. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Measured programming energy consumption is 6.1 nJ per epoch with the resistive switching PCM devices, a factor of ~150 times lower than conventional processor-memory systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Measured programming energy consumption is 6.1 nJ per epoch with the PCM devices, which is approximately 150 times lower than conventional processor-memory systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The energy measurement and comparative statement are reproduced verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Emerging memory technologies such as nanoscale two-terminal resistive switching memory devices offer a compact, scalable and low power alternative for on-chip co-located processing and memory. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claim list does not explicitly restate this general positioning claim about emerging memories as compact/scalable low-power alternatives, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metadata are not the same as ground truth, and treating them as such induces severe theoretical and practical problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Metadata are not the same as ground truth and treating them as such induces severe theoretical and practical problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this central conclusion verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No algorithm can uniquely solve community detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No algorithm can uniquely solve community detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result exactly, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There can be no algorithm that is optimal for all possible community detection tasks, as proven by a general No Free Lunch theorem for community detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There can be no algorithm that is optimal for all possible community detection tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Community detection remains a powerful tool and node metadata still have value, suggesting that a careful exploration of their relationship with network structure can yield insights of genuine worth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Community detection remains a powerful tool and node metadata still have value. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same sentiment but shortens the wording, retaining the conclusion that metadata have value; this is a mild clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Emergence effects, and not order effects, should be considered the main cognitive mechanism producing the observed conjunction fallacies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Emergence effects, and not order effects, should be considered the main cognitive mechanism producing the observed conjunction fallacies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same conclusion as the preprint, so there is no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conjunction fallacy can be modeled using quantum formalism. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conjunction fallacy can be modeled using quantum formalism. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in preprint and publication with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two major quantum approaches to understanding the conjunction fallacy have been proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two major quantum approaches have been proposed to explain the conjunction fallacy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed low-complexity approximation for the discrete Tchebichef transform (DTT) is multiplication-free and requires fewer additions and bit-shifting operations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed low-complex approximation for the DTT is multiplication free and requires a reduced number of additions and bit-shifting operations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Image and video compression simulations demonstrate good performance of the proposed transform. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hardware resource consumption for FPGA shows a 43.1% reduction of configurable logic blocks compared to the exact DTT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed transform shows a 43.1% reduction in configurable logic blocks for FPGA and a 57.7% reduction in the area-time figure for ASIC place and route realization compared with the 2D version of the exact DTT. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ASIC place and route realization of the proposed transform shows a 57.7% reduction in the area-time figure compared to the 2-D version of the exact DTT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed transform shows a 43.1% reduction in configurable logic blocks for FPGA and a 57.7% reduction in the area-time figure for ASIC place and route realization compared with the 2D version of the exact DTT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The ASIC area-time reduction is reproduced verbatim in the publication's combined hardware claim, so the claim content and confidence are maintained.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The discrete Tchebichef transform (DTT) possesses useful coding and decorrelation properties. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a general statement about intrinsic DTT properties that was not present among the preprint's explicit claims, so it is considered an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The principle 'Learning by Stimulation Avoidance' (LSA) allows for steering the dynamics of a biologically inspired neural network using carefully timed external stimulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The principle 'Learning by Stimulation Avoidance' (LSA) allows for steering the dynamics of a biologically inspired neural network using carefully timed external stimulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the LSA principle conclusion identically, so no change is detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The minimal sufficient conditions leading to LSA in artificial networks are sufficient to reproduce learning results similar to those obtained in biological neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The minimal sufficient conditions leading to LSA in artificial networks are sufficient to reproduce learning results similar to those obtained in biological neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication present this result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LSA has a higher explanatory power than existing hypotheses about the response of biological neural networks to external stimulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LSA has a higher explanatory power than existing hypotheses about the response of biological neural networks to external stimulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about LSA's explanatory power is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work demonstrates sensory-motor learning with random spiking networks through pure Hebbian learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research demonstrates sensory-motor learning with random spiking networks through Hebbian learning relying on environmental conditions without a separate reward system. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed spin based LIF spiking neuron using magneto-electric switching exhibits a typical leaky-integrate behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The voltage across the ME oxide demonstrates a typical leaky-integrate behavior that switches an underlying ferromagnet. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ferro-magnet in the proposed model demonstrates probabilistic switching dynamics due to thermal noise, resembling the stochasticity of biological neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ferromagnet's probabilistic switching dynamics, influenced by thermal noise, resemble the stochasticity of biological neurons. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The energy-efficiency of the magneto-electric switching mechanism results in lower energy consumption compared to a CMOS LIF neuron. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The energy efficiency of the ME switching mechanism results in lower energy consumption compared to a CMOS LIF neuron. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A device to system-level simulation framework has been developed to assess the proposed LIF neuron's feasibility for hand-written digit recognition. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed spin-based LIF spiking neuron utilizes magnetoelectric switching of ferromagnets to emulate biological neuron dynamics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a higher-level contribution statement about emulating biological dynamics via ME switching that is not explicitly stated as a distinct claim in the preprint, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed supervised dictionary learning algorithm preserves the local geometry in both dimensions of the data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed supervised dictionary learning algorithm preserves the local geometry in both dimensions of the data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of graph-based regularizations enhances the discriminative power of the learned sparse representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of two graph-based regularizations enhances the discriminative power of the learned sparse representations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method leads to improved classification accuracy compared to other dictionary-based approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method leads to improved classification accuracy compared to other dictionary-based approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm was evaluated on several datasets representing both single-label and multi-label classification problems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm was evaluated on several datasets representing both single-label and multi-label classification problems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is superior to the state-of-the-art methods in terms of robustness, accuracy, and efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is superior to the state-of-the-art methods in terms of robustness, accuracy, and efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative claim about superiority on robustness, accuracy, and efficiency.

alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution claim but provides concrete algorithmic details (energy function and least cost path), clarifying how the claimed robustness/accuracy is achieved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We present an efficient iterative algorithm to solve a large class of synchronization problems over compact groups.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The remaining cases show evidence of statistical-to-computational gaps.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state this decomposition of measurement error; it focuses on proposing and describing the deep learning approach rather than cataloguing error types.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial examples are mostly transferable across similar network topologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical claim about transferability across similar topologies.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Better machine learning models are less vulnerable to adversarial examples.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness of the tested machine learning models to adversarial examples was measured.

alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution and conclusion, with only minor wording changes (e.g., 'well-known') that do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Successful results indicate a perfect match of the trained model to the profiles of blood vessels in retinal images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful results indicate a perfect match of the trained model to the profiles of blood vessels in retinal images.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparisons among several statistical models obtained from different datasets reveal their high similarity, indicating they are independent of the dataset.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The best approximation of the statistical model with the symmetrized extension of the probabilistic model on the projective line bundle has a least square error smaller than 2%.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The engine uses alterations introduced by each platform as features for classification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method characterizes text networks by considering both topological and dynamical aspects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method characterizes text networks by considering both topological and dynamical aspects.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution directly and preserves its meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Thermal Gradient Flow method enhances the accuracy of nostril region tracking. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the contribution but shortens the description and lowers the stated certainty, thus clarifying and tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Thermal Voxel method increases the reliability of captured respiration signals compared to the traditional averaging method. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Thermal Voxel method increases the reliability of captured respiration signals compared to traditional averaging methods. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution with equivalent meaning but lower stated certainty and slightly reworded phrasing, so it is a clarification with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system demonstrates extreme robustness in tracking the nostril-region and measuring respiratory rate in high dynamic range scenes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrates extreme robustness in tracking the nostril-region and measuring the respiratory rate, with a strong correlation (r = 0.9987) with the ground truth from the respiration-belt sensor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific quantitative validation (r = 0.9987) supporting the robustness claim, clarifying and strengthening the empirical support (amplified confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Parsimonious Ensemble pENsemble offers a novel approach to ensemble learning that is better suited for data streams in complex environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Parsimonious Ensemble (pENsemble) classifier is built upon an evolving classifier from data streams, termed Parsimonious Classifier.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the contribution by situating pENsemble as built on a specific evolving classifier, which clarifies its foundation while preserving the original claim about a novel ensemble approach for data streams.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: pENsemble incorporates an ensemble pruning mechanism that estimates a localized generalization error of a base classifier.
alignments.pub_claim_text: pENsemble incorporates an ensemble pruning mechanism that estimates a localized generalization error of a base classifier.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in substance, but the publication expresses this with lower stated certainty (medium vs. high), so the substantive claim is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic online feature selection integrated into pENsemble allows for the real-time selection and deselection of input features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: pENsemble features a dynamic online feature selection mechanism that allows for the dynamic selection and deselection of input features.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanism and functionality with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of pENsemble has been numerically demonstrated to achieve a favourable tradeoff between accuracy and complexity in dynamic and evolving data streams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of pENsemble has been numerically demonstrated through rigorous studies, showing it achieves a favorable tradeoff between accuracy and complexity.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same empirical claim about demonstrating a favorable accuracy–complexity tradeoff; wording is slightly adjusted but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates a 40% performance increase in predicting participant notification attention compared to a random baseline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates a 40% performance increase in predicting participant notification attention compared to a random baseline.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact quantitative result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier achieves an AUC of 0.702 on a withheld test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier achieves an AUC of 0.702 on a withheld test set.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach allows forgoing resource-intensive, domain-specific, error-prone feature engineering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach allows forgoing resource-intensive, domain-specific, error-prone feature engineering.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about avoiding costly feature engineering is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method enhances the applicability of machine learning to mobile phone sensor data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is applicable to mobile phone sensor data, potentially increasing the applicability of machine learning in this domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly reframes wording but conveys the same applicability claim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method to predict protein function from sequence has been developed using deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have developed a novel method to predict protein function from sequence using deep learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the primary contribution of a novel deep-learning method for protein function prediction with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes a cross-species protein-protein interaction network and the structure of the Gene Ontology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes the dependencies between Gene Ontology classes as background information to construct a deep learning model.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes Gene Ontology class dependencies but omits explicit mention of cross-species PPI networks, indicating a narrowing/revision of the methodological description and lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates a significant improvement over baseline methods such as BLAST.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates a significant improvement over baseline methods such as BLAST for predicting cellular locations.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the improvement over BLAST pertains to predicting cellular locations, narrowing the scope of the original, more general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant improvement in predicting cellular locations using the proposed method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates a significant improvement over baseline methods such as BLAST for predicting cellular locations.
alignments.rationale: Both statements specifically claim improved performance for predicting cellular locations; the publication combines this with comparison to BLAST but does not change the asserted result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning strategy developed in this work predicts highly accurate molecular infrared spectra with unprecedented computational efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning strategy developed in this work predicts highly accurate molecular infrared spectra with unprecedented computational efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same principal result and claimed efficiency with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach overcomes the limitations of conventional quantum chemistry by accounting for vibrational anharmonic and dynamical effects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach overcomes the limitations of conventional quantum chemistry by accounting for vibrational anharmonic and dynamical effects.
alignments.rationale: Identical conceptual claim about addressing limitations of conventional methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Very accurate machine learning models for predicting infrared spectra can be obtained using only a few hundreds of electronic structure reference points.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Very accurate machine learning models for predicting infrared spectra can be obtained using only a few hundreds of electronic structure reference points.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this empirical claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning approach demonstrates excellent agreement between predicted infrared spectra and respective theoretical and experimental spectra.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning approach demonstrates excellent agreement between predicted infrared spectra and respective theoretical and experimental spectra.
alignments.rationale: The result about agreement with theory and experiment is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved a 1.72% Equal Error Rate on the GPDS-160 dataset, significantly improving upon the 6.97% reported in the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieved a 1.72% Equal Error Rate on the GPDS-160 dataset, significantly improving upon the 6.97% reported in the literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative performance improvement with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature learning process incorporates knowledge of skilled forgeries from a subset of users to enhance the distinction between genuine signatures and forgeries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature learning process incorporates knowledge of skilled forgeries from a subset of users to enhance the distinction between genuine signatures and forgeries.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is presented unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features learned from the proposed method generalize beyond the GPDS dataset, outperforming state-of-the-art performance on other datasets without fine-tuning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features learned from the proposed method generalize beyond the GPDS dataset, outperforming state-of-the-art performance on other datasets without fine-tuning.
alignments.rationale: The generalization and comparative performance claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.
